analysis.consistency: High. The data adheres to standard D&D 5e spell structure. Damage scaling is standard (levels 5, 11, 17). Components, range, and casting time are typical for a cantrip.
analysis.quality: High. The description is clear and mechanically precise. The specific effect on undead targets (disadvantage on attacks against the caster) adds unique utility for a necromancy cantrip.

mechanics.effect_duration: Until start of caster's next turn (HP regain prevention)
mechanics.secondary_effect: If target is undead, disadvantage on attack rolls against caster until end of caster's next turn.

analysis.notes: This spell serves as excellent early-game damage paired with strong defensive utility against undead, which aligns well with the Necromancy school and its primary users (Warlocks/Wizards).
